visual_checks: Look for live audio-only content such as live radio broadcasts, audio streams, or live podcasts
Check if a real-time text alternative (such as live captions or a live transcript) is provided alongside the audio
Verify the text alternative updates in near real-time as the live audio plays
Check if the text alternative is clearly associated with the live audio stream
Look for a link or embedded region displaying the live text alternative
Verify the text alternative captures all spoken content, not just summaries
pass_conditions: A real-time text alternative is provided for all live audio-only content
The text alternative updates in near real-time as the live audio plays
All spoken content in the live audio is captured in the text alternative
The text alternative is clearly associated with its live audio source
The text alternative is presented in an accessible format readable by assistive technologies
fail_conditions: Live audio-only content has no real-time text alternative provided
A text alternative exists but is significantly delayed behind the live audio
The text alternative only provides summaries rather than full content of the live audio
The text alternative is not clearly linked to or associated with the live audio stream
The text alternative is presented in an inaccessible format
na_conditions: Page contains no live audio-only content
All audio content on the page is prerecorded rather than live
The media is synchronized video-with-audio rather than audio-only
ne_conditions: AI response could not be parsed
Screenshots could not be captured for visual verification
Live audio stream could not be accessed so text alternatives cannot be evaluated
common_mistakes: Providing a transcript only after the live event ends instead of in real time
Real-time text alternative that only covers portions of the live audio content
Live text alternative placed on a separate page without clear navigation from the audio player
Relying on automated speech-to-text with high error rates and no human correction
Text alternative that summarizes content rather than providing full real-time transcription

examples.pass: A live audio news broadcast includes a synchronized live text feed displayed below the audio player that updates in real time with all spoken content
examples.fail: A live radio stream on the website has no text alternative; a transcript is only made available hours after the broadcast ends
examples.partial: A live podcast provides real-time captions but they frequently lag by 30+ seconds and miss significant portions of the spoken content

plain_meaning: At Level AAA, live audio-only content (a live radio-style stream with no video) must have a time-based media alternative that conveys the same information. In practice this usually means live captions or a live transcript. The intent is that deaf and hard-of-hearing users can follow live audio broadcasts.
scope_applies_to: Live audio-only streams embedded on the page, when AAA conformance is claimed
Live radio-style broadcasts with no video component
Live audio feeds of events with no visual component
scope_does_not_apply_to: Live synchronized media (live video with audio) -- that belongs to SC 1.2.4
Prerecorded audio-only content -- that belongs to SC 1.2.1
Keyboard operability of the player -- that belongs to SC 2.1.1
Missing label on the player launch button -- that belongs to SC 4.1.2
borderline_calibration: Live captions or a live transcript both satisfy the rule.
If no live audio-only content is present on the page, mark not-applicable.
This is AAA -- only evaluate on AAA claims or explicit request.
Missing alternative on live audio-only in AAA context is a hard failure at medium+.
